seo.og_title: Logto or Descope for Next.js? Our Honest Comparison
seo.og_description: We compared Logto and Descope head-to-head. Pricing: Free – $599/mo vs Free – $2,000/mo. Read our full analysis.

hero.headline: Logto vs Descope for Next.js Authentication
hero.subheadline: An honest comparison of Logto and Descope — pricing, DX, setup time, and which one we recommend for Next.js App Router projects in 2026.

quick_summary: Logto vs Descope is a common comparison we hear from Next.js developers. Logto (4.1★) and Descope (4.2★) both have loyal followings. Our analysis shows Logto has the edge for most Next.js projects due to its 7/10 DX score. Descope is a strong alternative if yes, up to 5,000 mau with 2,000 transactions better matches your budget.
verdict_text: We recommend Logto for most Next.js App Router projects. Its medium learning curve and 20 minutes setup time make it immediately productive. Descope is a worthy alternative, particularly if you need yes, up to 5,000 mau with 2,000 transactions or prefer Descope's approach to authentication.

User-friendly admin dashboard for non-technical team members
cons: Smaller community and fewer third-party integrations
Next.js SDK is less mature with fewer examples
Self-hosted version requires significant infrastructure setup

pros: Visual drag-and-drop workflow builder for authentication flows
No-code screens reduce front-end development time
Built-in analytics and user journey tracking
Support for passkeys, biometrics, and passwordless auth
cons: Expensive at scale - pricing jumps at 10K MAU
Visual builder adds abstraction that can be limiting for custom flows
Smaller community and less third-party integration

faq.question: Should I choose Logto or Descope for my Next.js app?
faq.answer: It depends on your specific needs. Logto scores 7/10 for Next.js integration with a Medium learning curve. Descope scores 7/10 with a Medium learning curve. For most projects, we recommend Logto because of its stronger DX and better alignment with App Router patterns. However, if Yes, up to 5,000 MAU or Yes, up to 5,000 MAU with 2,000 transactions is a deciding factor, the cheaper option may serve you well.

faq.question: Is Logto or Descope more affordable?
faq.answer: Logto starts at Free – $599/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU. Descope starts at Free – $2,000/mo with a free tier of Yes, up to 5,000 MAU with 2,000 transactions. The total cost of ownership depends on your MAU (monthly active users). At lower volumes, the free tiers of both are sufficient. As you scale, Logto offers better value at scale. Factor in hidden costs like enterprise features, SSO, and database infrastructure when comparing.

faq.question: Is Logto or Descope easier to set up with Next.js?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Descope has a Medium learning curve with an estimated setup time of 20 minutes. Descope is faster to set up, but ease of integration also depends on your team's familiarity with the framework and whether you want pre-built UI components or prefer to build your own.

faq.question: Can I use Logto and Descope together?
faq.answer: Using both Logto and Descope in the same Next.js application is not recommended. They would create separate session management systems, conflicting middleware, and competing cookies. Choose one auth provider and stick with it. Migrating between auth providers later is possible but requires user data export and rebuilding authentication flows.

faq.question: Which has better developer experience: Logto or Descope?
faq.answer: Logto has a Medium learning curve, a Next.js integration score of 7/10, and a user rating of 4.1★. Descope has a Medium learning curve, 7/10, and 4.2★. On paper, Logto leads in DX. In practice, your specific requirements around pre-built components, customization, and team expertise matter just as much. Try both free tiers to see what clicks.
